trackingsummarydata.subject: This week in A&S: Recycling cells, small figures and scholarship winners
trackingsummarydata.title: FSSG - 02/18/25

urls.page_title: Museum installation focuses on small figures in large landscapes | Cornell Chronicle
urls.page_summary: A new student-led installation at the Herbert F. Johnson Museum of Art explores how the figures, known as “staffage,” indicate scale in paintings and also tell larger stories about the art.
urls.page_title: Cornell researchers win prize for insight into conspiracy belief
urls.page_summary: Gordon Pennycook and David Rand won the 2026 Newcomb Cleveland Prize from the American Association for the Advancement of Science.
Awards and honors received by faculty, postdocs and graduate students in the College of Arts & Sciences.
urls.page_title: Newly identified protein interaction helps keep cells’ recycling system in balance | Cornell Chronicle
urls.page_summary: Cornell researchers have discovered a new way cells regulate how they respond to stress, identifying an interaction between two proteins that helps keep a critical cellular recycling system in balance.
urls.page_title: Bad Bunny performance sparks buzz for vintage Ford truck
urls.page_summary: Bad Bunny featured a vintage Ford F-100 pickup last week, giving the automaker millions in free advertising as he connected with his Puerto Rican roots.

Join the Department of Performing and Media Arts for the 13th Annual Aster Film Festival on Saturday and Sunday, February 21-22, from 1:00 PM - 6:00 PM on each day, in the Film Forum, Schwartz Center for the Performing Arts. Screenings will take p...
Presenting the world's greatest musical traditions since 1903.
urls.page_title: Events honor the legacy of composer Steven Stucky
urls.page_summary: The Department of Music is honoring the late Steven Stucky, Pulitzer Prize-winning composer and beloved Cornell professor, with a series of concerts.
urls.page_title: The Richard Cleaveland Memorial Reading: Featuring Dante Micheaux and Alex Gilvarry - Cornell
urls.page_summary:
The Richard Cleaveland Memorial Reading by Dante Micheaux and Alex Gilvarry Thursday, February 19, 5 p.m. Rhodes-Rawlings Auditorium, Klarman Hall KG70
The Spring 2026 Barbara & David Zalaznick Creative Writing Reading Series kicks off with the R...
